title: quinquina obovata
intro: Quinquina obovata, also known as Cinchona obovata, is a tropical plant species native to the Andean regions of South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant's bark has been used to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Quinquina obovata include reducing fever, treating malaria, and alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is typically dried and processed into teas, tinctures, or capsules, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
